feed.title: Oliver Aleksander Larsen — Research Papers
feed.subtitle: Software architecture research at IEEE ICSA 2026

entry.title: Edge-Based Standing-Water Detection via FSM-Guided Tiering and Multi-Model Consensus
entry.summary: Standing water detection on agricultural edge devices (Raspberry Pi + Jetson). We present an FSM-guided control plane that selects between local and offloaded inference tiers based on resource availability and environmental conditions. A multi-model YOLO ensemble combined with diurnal sensor fusion achieves robust detection across varying lighting and weather conditions, enabling real-time flood monitoring on resource-constrained hardware.

category.term: IEEE ICSA 2026, Software Architecture in Practice Track

entry.title: RAD-AI: Rethinking Architecture Documentation for AI-Augmented Ecosystems
entry.summary: We propose a backward-compatible extension of arc42 (adding 8 sections) and C4 (adding 3 diagram types) specifically designed for AI-augmented ecosystems. Our extensions enable systematic documentation of AI components, their data flows, training pipelines, and regulatory requirements. When evaluated against EU AI Act Annex IV requirements, RAD-AI improves documentation addressability from approximately 36% to 93%. We validate the framework on two industrial case studies: Uber Michelangelo and Netflix Metaflow.

entry.title: BitSov: A Composable Bitcoin-Native Architecture for Sovereign Internet Infrastructure
entry.summary: We present BitSov, an eight-layer composable architectural framework for Bitcoin-native sovereign internet infrastructure. The framework introduces three architectural patterns: payment-gated messaging, timechain-locked contracts, and a self-sustaining economic flywheel. BitSov employs dual settlement across on-chain Bitcoin transactions and Lightning Network micropayments, enabling censorship-resistant communication and identity management without reliance on traditional internet infrastructure.

entry.title: SAGAI-MID: A Generative AI-Driven Middleware for Dynamic Runtime Interoperability
entry.summary: We present SAGAI-MID, a FastAPI-based middleware that uses large language models to dynamically resolve schema mismatches at runtime. The system implements a five-layer pipeline with hybrid detection, dual resolution strategies (DIRECT transformation vs. CODEGEN), and a three-tier safeguard stack. Evaluated across 10 interoperability scenarios using 6 different LLMs, the best configuration achieves 0.90 pass@1 accuracy, demonstrating practical viability of LLM-driven runtime schema adaptation.

entry.title: Hybrid Large-Language-Model Meal Recommender System for Athletes: A Short Communication
entry.summary: Athletes often struggle with static, rule-based meal plans that fail to adapt to evolving personal preferences and feedback, leading to reduced adherence and suboptimal nutrition for performance. This short communication introduces a hybrid large-language-model (LLM)-driven recommender system to personalize meal recommendations within CarboPlanner, a sports nutrition platform. The system combines in-context learning (ICL) for short-term adaptation via dynamic prompts incorporating user profiles, recent feedback, and meal candidates, with parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) using low-rank adaptation (LoRA) on the Mistral-7B-Instruct model for long-term, system-wide learning from accumulated feedback. Implemented as a modular Flask API integrated seamlessly into CarboPlanner's existing infrastructure (Flutter app, NestJS backend, and MongoDB database), the system processes athlete feedback through dual loops: immediate ICL updates and periodic PEFT retraining. Evaluation on 1000 test prompts from a held-out dataset demonstrated superior performance over baselines. The hybrid model achieved 46.7% prediction accuracy and 100% format adherence, outperforming the non-fine-tuned ICL-only model (41.5% accuracy, 39.7% format adherence) and random selection (20% accuracy, 100% format adherence).
